title: Reported questions and commands
explanation:
Reported questions and commands

In reported questions, we use statement word order and usually backshift the tense: He asked where I lived. For commands and requests, we use tell / ask + object + to + verb: She told me to wait.

questions.text: He asked me where I [*lived|live|did I live].
questions.feedback: Reported questions use statement word order.

questions.text: She wanted to know if I [*had finished|finished|have finished] the report.
The teacher told us [*to be|be|being] quiet.
My mother asked me [*not to stay|to not stay|not stay] out late.

questions.feedback: Whether or if introduces reported yes/no questions.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: He asked ____ I was late. | *why | that | to
She told me ____ the door behind me. | *to lock | locking | lock
The manager asked us ____ the customers. | *to call | calling | call
I asked him ____ he needed any help. | *whether | that | what
She asked me what time the train [*left|did leave|leaves].
questions.feedback: No question word order in reported speech.

questions.text: He told the children [*not to run|to not run|not run] in the corridor.
The doctor asked whether I [*was feeling|am feeling|felt to be] better.
We wanted to know where they [*had gone|did go|have gone].
The note told visitors [*to switch off|switch off|switching off] their phones.
